name: Caramel Crunch Pecan Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Dark brown sugar, firmly packed
1/2 cup Whipping cream plus 1 tbsp Whipping cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Grease a jellyroll pan with butter-flavored Crisco.
3. Combine brown sugar, salt, butter, boiling water, and vinegar in a large saucepan. Stir until well blended.
4. Cook and stir over medium heat until the mixture comes to a boil. Cover and boil 2 to 3 minutes.
5. Uncover and brush brown sugar crystals with wet pastry brush. Boil slowly without stirring until the mixture reaches the hard-crack stage (295 to 310°F on a candy thermometer).
6. Stir gently without touching the sides of the saucepan. Pour into the pan, spreading if necessary. Cool to room temperature.
7. Break into squares and break up enough squares to make 3 tablespoons small bits or pieces. Cover and store the remainder for eating later.
8. Combine flour and salt in a medium-sized bowl. Cut in Crisco until all flour is blended to form pea-sized chunks.
9. Sprinkle with ice water, 1 tablespoon at a time. Toss lightly with a fork until dough forms a ball.
10. Divide dough into two. Press between hands to form two 5- to 6-inch "pancakes".
11. Roll one dough for the bottom crust into a circle between sheets of plastic wrap. Peel off the top sheet and trim pastry 1 inch larger than an upside-down 9-inch pie plate. Fit into the pie plate.
12. Press to fit, remove the other sheet of plastic wrap. Freeze for 15 minutes.
13. Roll second "pancake" the same as for the crust. Freeze for 15 minutes.
14. Heat oven to 400°F (200°C). Cut out 26 to 28 leaf shapes and 3 larger maple-leaf shapes from the rolled pastry.
15. Arrange leaves in an overlapping and slightly curved pattern on a baking sheet. Brush with whipping cream.
16. Moisten tips of small leaves with ice water. Press around the edge of the crust in an overlapping fashion. Brush with cream.
17. Bake leaves at 400°F (200°C) for 10 minutes, or until lightly browned.
18. Combine granulated sugar, salt, corn syrup, eggs, pancake-waffle syrup, melted butter, vanilla, and molasses in a medium-sized bowl. Beat with a whisk until blended.
19. Stir in nuts. Pour into the unbaked pie shell. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 10 minutes.
20. Reduce temperature to 350°F (175°C). Bake for 25 to 35 minutes, or until filling is firm when gently shaken.
21. Cover edge to keep from overbrowning.
22. Combine granulated sugar and water in a small saucepan. Cook and stir over low heat until the sugar dissolves.
23. Increase heat to medium. Boil without stirring for 11 minutes until the mixture reaches a deep amber color, brushing down sugar crystals with a wet pastry brush.
24. Remove from heat. Stir in whipping cream; mixture will bubble up. Add butter, stirring until smooth. Cook and stir over low heat about 3 minutes or until the color deepens and sauce thickens slightly.
25. Spoon half of the sauce over the warm pie. Cool for an hour. Cover and refrigerate remaining sauce for ice cream topping.
26. Sprinkle top with toffee bits. Place leaf decoration on one side of the pie. Refrigerate for 4 hours before serving. Refrigerate leftovers. Makes a 9-inch pie.
